steps.title: Identify the goal
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize a difference between winged kelp (moderate umami) and arame (high umami). The correct choice must contrast the two intensities.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — “Both winged kelp and arame are types of brown seaweed”: States a similarity, not a difference — no contrast between umami intensities. ✗
  • Choice B — “Winged kelp and arame both contain umami flavor…”: Again emphasizes similarity (“both contain”) and adds irrelevant information about pork and soy — no contrast of intensity. ✗
  • Choice C — “Participants found winged kelp…moderate umami intensity, while arame’s umami was more intense”: Directly contrasts moderate (winged kelp) vs. more intense (arame) — fits the goal of highlighting a difference. ✓
  • Choice D — “Some types of brown seaweed, like winged kelp and arame, trigger umami…”: Groups both seaweeds together rather than contrasting them. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
